title: The Place of the Skull
subtitle: The Sixth Hour: led outside the gate to Calvary, given wine mingled with gall

stillness: Be still now, sorrowing and sad.
Gather of the wood of the cross, and the fire of devotion will be kindled in you again.
gospel: he went forth to that place called the Skull,
And they gave him wine to drink, mingled with myrrh,
And when he had tasted it, he would not drink.
They gave me gall for my food,
and in my thirst they gave me vinegar to drink.
behold: Now behold the wicked workmen, on every side, working their malice.
Look carefully at each thing done against your Lord, and each thing said;
and the things done by him, and through him.
Make yourself present to it all with the whole gaze of your mind.
See, then, with the eyes of your mind:
when they had seen him already worn out, still they mocked him.
They gave him wine mingled with gall —
seeking even to embitter the inmost heart of the most sweet Lord,
He tasted a little, that what was written might be fulfilled.
consider: Consider why the death of Christ was the most shameful of deaths.
From the place: where evildoers and the guilty were punished.
From the manner: for crucifixion was the most disgraceful kind of death.
From his company: crucified between two criminals, as though a guilty man himself.
Thus the Jews meant to make the people suspect he shared in their crimes; in all things they meant to make him vile.
Yet that cross, once the punishment of robbers and of much disgrace,
has now passed from the places of execution to the brows of Emperors, and is an immeasurable glory.
Here Christ is made bald — that is, cut off from his own flesh.
He suffered outside the gate, that you may know the sacrifice is common, the offering of the whole earth, the purification of all.
And outside the gate he suffered, that whoever would follow the fruit of his Passion must go out of this world, at least in heart.
For, as Gregory says: a man is near to God in the measure that he is a stranger to the love of the world.
This holy mountain holds great dignity among the holy places, and the power to pierce the heart.
When the fire of devotion is dead in you, go there;
and as in the wine-press of the Passion, the tears of compassion are pressed out.
prayer: Lord Jesus, they gave you gall for your food,
and in your thirst they gave you vinegar to drink;
they sought to embitter the inmost heart of you, most sweet Lord.
You suffered outside the gate — let me go out to you.
Outside the camp, outside the city of this world,
bearing your reproach and your pain.
Let me leave the world in love, that I may not love it;
leave it in deed, forsaking it wholly in mind and body;
that, going forward, I may pant to become one spirit with you.
For a man is near to you, my God, in the measure that he is a stranger to the love of the world.

latinJewel.latin: Ubi prius erat area damnatorum, ibi erigerentur vexilla Martyrum, et ubi abundavit peccatum, superabundaret gratia Dei.
latinJewel.english: Where before was the field of the condemned, there the banners of the Martyrs would be raised; and where sin abounded, the grace of God would more abound.
